commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is indicated; the patch appears to address the prior concrete finding, but the PR is still draft and maintainer-labeled.; Cleared: The diff is limited to memory-core warning logic and tests; it does not change dependencies, workflows, package scripts, credentials, permissions, or artifact execution paths. (sha=eb4fda1755ecca9dcf4545d3cf2421505dd71975)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds memory-core runtime warnings when built-in, native-fallback, and QMD watchers are already tracking more than 2,000 directories or paths, with focused watcher tests.

PR surface: Source +105, Tests +110. Total +215 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for review purposes: current main lacks the warning path, and the related memory-core FD-pressure report documents the large-watcher pressure this PR targets. I did not run a live FD-pressure reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; the patch appears to address the prior concrete finding, but the PR is still draft and maintainer-labeled.

target.title: fix(memory): warn on async runtime watcher pressure
target.body:
## Summary

- re-add the memory-watch FD pressure warning after #89246 reverted the earlier warning-only PR
- keep memory watching enabled by default and warn only when runtime watcher state is already large enough to be risky
- move the warning out of config validation so normal startup config loading does not recursively scan memory folders
- cover built-in memory watching, QMD collection watching, and the existing-chokidar native-fallback path with focused watcher tests

## ClawSweeper feedback addressed

- fixed the missed warning path when a native directory watcher fails at runtime and the large memory directory is added to an existing chokidar watcher
- deferred and bounded the fallback pressure check so chokidar has time to populate `getWatched()` after async `add()` work
- clear the deferred pressure-check timer during manager shutdown

## Related

- Replaces the warning behavior reverted by #89246.
- Follow-up to #89185, but now self-contained because #89185 was reverted.
- Refs #86613, where FD pressure was reproduced with large watched memory trees.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/validation.policy.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.policy.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
target.author: romneyda
